query: Recent PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan ADIZ in May-June 2026, and reported pause or drop in activity. What are typical daily counts recently and highest single-day count in 2026?
answer:
I've completed my research on PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan's ADIZ in May–June 2026. Here are the key findings:

## Key Findings

**The reported drop/pause:**
- Air activity in H1 2026 is at **multi-year lows** — every month Jan–Jun 2026 was the lowest for that respective month since at least 2023.
- A notable **~week-long pause (Feb 27–Mar 5, 2026)** saw *zero* PLA aircraft near the ADIZ, described as unusually long and rare. Analysts (Taiwan Security Monitor/GMU) attributed it mostly to routine variability, with possible NPC/festival, pre-summit, and fuel-conservation factors.
- March 2026 fell to ~171 sorties, the lowest monthly total since the August 2022 post-Pelosi surge.

**Typical recent daily counts (May–June 2026):**
- Generally **single digits to low-to-mid 20s**. Examples: May 1 (4), May 18 (13), May 28 (10), Jun 6 (22), Jun 13 (6), Jun 25 (2), Jul 6 (4).
- June 2026 monthly total: **214 sorties / 134 median-line crossings**, well below June 2024–2025.

**Highest single-day count in 2026:**
- **~36 aircraft on March 17, 2026** appears to be the 2026 peak. No 100+ aircraft encirclement-drill day occurred in H1 2026.
- **Important caveat:** The frequently-cited 130-aircraft/90-crossing record day (2nd-highest ever) was **December 30, 2025** during "Justice Mission-2025" — *not* in 2026.

**Notable divergence:** While air sorties dropped, **naval activity surged** — June 2026's 225 PLAN vessels was the highest June on record — suggesting a shift in the composition of pressure rather than an overall reduction. Full structured data with sources has been submitted.

sources.title: All Quiet in the Taiwan Strait? Explaining the Recent Drop in PLA Aircraft Activity Around Taiwan
sources.snippet: From Feb 27 to Mar 5, 2026, MND reported no PLA aircraft near the ADIZ; describes it as an unusually long pause; cites daily counts Jan 29 (19), Feb 12 (32), Feb 26 (22).

sources.title: K. Tristan Tang – Monthly Update on PLA Air and Maritime Maneuvers Around Taiwan (June 2026)
sources.snippet: June 2026: 134 median line crossings and 214 total aircraft sorties; 225 PLAN vessels (highest June on record); one ~21.5h patrol.

sources.title: March 2026 PLA aircraft sorties around Taiwan
sources.snippet: March 2026 total 171 sorties, lowest since Aug 2022 (800); peak of 36 aircraft on March 17, 2026.

sources.title: K. Tristan Tang – Monthly Update (March 2026)
sources.snippet: 173 aircraft sorties in March 2026, lowest monthly figure since 2023; Feb 2026 second-lowest; two consecutive months of reduced activity.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (May 28-29, 2026)
sources.snippet: May 28-29, 2026: 10 sorties of PLA aircraft, all 10 crossed median line; 8 PLAN ships, 4 official ships.

sources.title: PLA activities around Taiwan (May 18-19, 2026)
sources.snippet: May 18-19, 2026: 13 sorties, 10 crossed median line; 5 PLAN ships, 2 official ships.

sources.title: PLA activities around Taiwan (Apr 30 - May 1, 2026)
sources.snippet: Apr 30-May 1, 2026: 4 sorties, all 4 entered ADIZ; 5 PLAN ships, 2 official ships.

sources.title: Taiwan tracks 22 military aircraft, 10 Chinese ships (Jun 6, 2026)
sources.snippet: Jun 6, 2026: 22 PLA aircraft, 8 naval vessels, 2 official ships; 2 of 22 entered central/SW ADIZ.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 6 PLA aircraft sorties, 8 PLAN vessels (Jun 13, 2026)
sources.snippet: Jun 13, 2026: 6 sorties, 4 into SW ADIZ; 8 PLAN vessels, 2 official ships.

sources.title: PLA activities around Taiwan (Jun 25-26, 2026)
sources.snippet: Jun 25-26, 2026: 2 sorties of PLA aircraft and 6 PLAN ships; both sorties entered SW ADIZ.

sources.title: PLA activities around Taiwan (Jul 6-7, 2026)
sources.snippet: Jul 6-7, 2026: 4 sorties, 3 crossed median line; 9 PLAN ships, 3 official ships.

sources.title: Taiwan Security Monitor – 90 median-line crossings / 130 aircraft (Dec 30, 2025)
sources.snippet: Dec 30, 2025: 130 PLA aircraft, 90 crossed median line — second-highest one-day total on record (occurred 2025, not 2026).

sources.title: Tracking China's Increased Military Activities in the Indo-Pacific in 2025
sources.snippet: ChinaPower/CSIS analysis (Feb 5, 2026) of 2025 PLA activity trends around Taiwan.

sources.title: PLA Sorties Around Taiwan: Fewer Aircraft, Higher Threat
sources.snippet: Analysis of declining aircraft counts amid higher-threat joint combat readiness patrols.

March 2026 total PLA aircraft sorties (lowest since Aug 2022)

Consecutive days with zero PLA aircraft near ADIZ (Feb 27-Mar 5, 2026 pause)
